dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5967 IH: Strategic Task Force on Scam Prevention Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Menendez (for himself, Mrs. Houchin, Ms. Craig, Mrs. Dingell, Ms. Norton, Mr. Whitesides, Ms. McClellan, Mr. Thompson of California, Ms. Kelly of Illinois, Mr. Carter of Louisiana, and Mr. Subramanyam) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To direct the Federal Trade Commission, in conjunction with the Department of Justice, to convene an interagency task force for the purpose of addressing scams, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Strategic Task Force on Scam Prevention Act.

subsection.text: The Federal Trade Commission, in conjunction with the Department of Justice, shall convene an interagency task force for the purpose of addressing scams.
The task force convened under subsection (a) shall include representatives from each of the following:

paragraph.text: The Department of Homeland Security, including the United States Secret Service.
The Department of Justice, including the Federal Bureau of Investigation.
The task force convened under subsection (a) shall develop and implement a national strategy to address scams that includes—

clause.text: the Consumer Sentinel Network of the Federal Trade Commission; and
the Internet Crime Complaint Center of the Federal Bureau of Investigation;

subparagraph.text: coordination with industry entities, including providers of online platforms;
enforcement actions using existing authorities, including authorities with respect to money laundering, human trafficking, and fraud;
strategies to reduce the systemic risk of financial losses as a result of scams and improve recovery resources for victims of scams.

paragraph.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the task force convened under subsection (a) shall submit to Congress, and make available to the public, a report on the actions taken by the task force pursuant to paragraph (1).

subsection.text: In carrying out the duties specified in subsection (c), the task force convened under subsection (a) shall consult with representatives of the following:

paragraph.text: Consumer advocacy organizations that address fraud and scams.
Industry, including the following industry sectors:
State securities commissions (including State agencies and offices that perform similar functions).

subsection.text: The task force convened under subsection (a) shall terminate on the date that is 10 years after the date of the enactment of this Act.
